text.content:
You will pick up most of this by playing. Keep this page for when something on the rest window confuses you.

What happens during a long rest

The GM sets the scene. You pick one camp activity. The GM rolls for the night. Then the rest finishes and your character recovers.

Comfort tiers

These apply when the GM runs comfort rules (Survival Quick Setup, or Comfort Rules on under Recovery Rules). The rest window shows a comfort badge. It is the biggest factor in how much you recover when comfort is on. Hover the badge any time to see the exact numbers.

TierWhat it costs you
SafeFull HP and Hit Dice back. No risk of a night event.
ShelteredFull HP and Hit Dice back. A normal night.
RoughFull HP, 1 fewer Hit Die back. CON save DC 10 or +1 exhaustion.
HostileRegain 75% of max HP from the rest, 2 fewer Hit Dice back. Existing exhaustion sticks. CON save DC 15 or +1 exhaustion.

Gear that helps

When comfort rules are on, these items on your sheet are picked up automatically. Hover any item for its rest effect.

  • Bedroll: +1 Hit Die back during a long rest.
  • Mess kit or Cook's utensils: advantage on the exhaustion save (only when the fire is lit).
  • Tent: shelter; reduces weather penalties and makes the camp harder for enemies to find.
  • Tinderbox: lets the party light the fire. One per group is enough.

Your camp activity

You pick one. Shared duties (Keep Watch, Set Up Defenses, Tend Wounds) help the whole camp when encounters are on. Personal options (Rest Fully, Train, Forage, Fletch Arrows, Cook, Identify, Attune) help only you. Caster characters get arcane options when they apply.

Rest Fully bumps your personal comfort up one tier but leaves you in deep sleep if the night turns. Cook uses your slot to feed the party and may grant a Well Fed buff. Train runs three drills in one rest, each its own check, and pays XP for every set you land plus a little for the ones you miss. It only helps through level 5, and training on back-to-back rests returns less each night, so space it out or switch activities to reset it.

Travel scouting (Survival setup)

When the GM uses the Survival Quick Setup, or turns on Travel Scouting under Rest Activities, the last travel day before camp may offer Scout alongside forage and hunt. Choose Scout, roll Perception or Survival (whichever modifier is higher on your sheet), and wait. You will not see the result. The GM describes the campsite from the roll.

More than one character can scout. Only the best roll counts for the party.

The fire

When comfort rules are on, someone tends the fire. Embers are quiet. A campfire is normal. A bonfire raises camp comfort one step but is visible from far off; enemies spot the camp easily. Cold camp (no fire) drops camp comfort but is hardest for enemies to spot.

Events

The GM rolls against an encounter DC that the party's choices and the terrain shape together. Sometimes nothing happens. Sometimes it does. Keep Watch and Set Up Defenses make it less likely enemies surprise the camp when an encounter triggers.
